title: Bumble Inc. Announces Leadership Transition: Whitney Wolfe Herd Returns as CEO
text: In January 2025, Bumble announced that founder Whitney Wolfe Herd would return as CEO in mid-March, replacing Lidiane Jones, who was stepping down for personal reasons. A year after assuming the role and initiating a slew of controversial changes, Lidiane Jones is out as CEO of Bumble Inc. In her short time as CEO, she led an overhaul of the app, which changed how men and women initiate conversations on the app. The company's stock cratered further in August after quarterly revenue and guidance for Q3 of 2024 came in below estimates. Shares were down by around 54% from the time Bumble allowed men to message first to the end of 2024. It's a return to form for Wolfe Herd, who founded the dating app company at age 24 and led it for nine years. Bumble founder Whitney Wolfe Herd is returning as CEO, emphasizing her commitment to restoring confidence and joy to the dating experience through improved matching and safety features. The leadership transition represents a strategic pivot as the company faces ongoing challenges related to user growth and revenue deceleration.
